pages.text:
MLS NEXT ACADEMY DIVISION
RULES & REGULATIONS
2025-2026

I. OVERVIEW
Operators, as licensees of Player Development, L.L.C. (“PDev”) (which is a subsidiary of Major
League Soccer, L.L.C. (“MLS”)) operate territories of MLS NEXT Academy Division (the
“Program” or “League” operating as “MLS NEXT Academy Division”) with the mission of
providing training, development, education, and competition opportunities for select soccer
players between the age groups of U13 to U19 across the United States. The Program includes a
league, postseason competition, as well as other development-focused initiatives.
II. DEFINITIONS
Capitalized terms shall have the definitions ascribed to them in this section. Capitalized terms not
defined in this section but defined in other sections of these Rules and Regulations or in other
Rules and Policies (as defined below) will have the meaning ascribed to them in those sections or
documents.
a. Club (or “Member”). Any soccer club whose application for membership in MLS NEXT
Academy Division has been accepted, and that has signed a Membership Agreement and
paid a specified performance bond.
b. Club Representative. Any Club personnel, including but not limited to, any Club
employees, Coaches, trainers, Team administrators, other support or volunteer staff, or
any individual who represents themselves as being affiliated with a Club in a manner that
is known or should be known to the Club, that assist the Club in connection with the
Season.
c. Coach. An individual who performs the duties and services as may be assigned to an
individual by a Club regarding the training of youth soccer players, including, but not
limited to, (i) conducting and supervising the Team’s practices, training camps, and
training sessions, (ii) coaching the Team in all competitions and Matches, (iii) preparing
for all Matches played by the Team, including developing Match strategies, (iv)
overseeing off-season Player development programs for the Team, (v) consulting and
advising with respect to the Team’s Roster, (vi) directing and supervising the Team’s
coaching staff, (vii) enforcing Club and League rules, and/or (viii) participating in
promotional, sponsorship, and public relations activities.
d. Competitive Season. The period of time between the first official Match scheduled by
Operator for the Conference in which the Club is registered and the final official Match
scheduled by that Operator (including MLS NEXT Cup).
e. Conference. The grouping of Teams that compete against each other in the Regular
Season and whose performance and standings are tracked and reported together.
f. Facility. A venue for Team training and Matches, including all parking, structures, and
grounds on or immediately surrounding the venue.
1

g. FIFA. The Fédération Internationale de Football Association (International Federation of
Association Football), the international governing body of association soccer.
h. Fixture. A set of scheduled Matches of multiple age groups versus one opponent.
i. Forfeit. A 3-0 loss for the team that is issued the forfeiture for a Match.
j. Infraction. Any violation or alleged violation of the Rules and Policies, or any action or
alleged action that is against the spirit of the Rules and Policies.
k. Lifetime Ban. Permanently prohibiting an individual from being a Participant.
l. Match/Game. Any soccer match played between two Clubs or any match organized by
an Operator as part of an MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition.
m. Match Day Roster. The official match day roster that each Team must create and submit
to the referee prior to a Match, which for U13 through U19 lists 20 Players from the
Roster who will be eligible to participate in that Match as well as all individuals who will
be in the Club’s technical area at any point during the Match.
n. Match Decision. Events or matters that have a direct effect on Matches, excluding
disciplinary matters, including, but not limited to, the eligibility of Players, the state of
the field, accessory Match equipment, crowd behavior, and stadium installations.
o. Match Official. The referee, assistant referees, fourth official, Match commissioner,
referee inspector, the person in charge of safety, and/or any other persons appointed by
Operator and/or Professional Referee Organization (“PRO”) to assume responsibility in
connection with a Match.
p. Match Report. A form submitted by a Match Official that records Match data on the field
of play, including, but not limited to, a win, loss, tie, goals scored for, goals scored
against, total points achieved, misconduct, suspensions, and injuries.
q. Match Suspension. A ban that prevents a Player, Club Representative (in the technical
area), or other Participant from taking part in any future Match or MLS NEXT Academy
Division Competition, including, but not limited to, being present in the area immediately
surrounding the field of play. The suspension is imposed in terms of Matches, days, or
months.
r. Membership Agreement. The document that details the rights and obligations of each
Club; a fully executed Membership Agreement is required for a Club to become a valid
member of MLS NEXT Academy Division.
s. MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition. The MLS NEXT Academy Division
schedule, collectively referring to friendly matches, Regular Season matches, MLS
2

NEXT Fest, MLS NEXT Academy Division Regional Events, and MLS NEXT Cup
(each as applicable), featuring at least one Club and organized by Operator and/or PDev.
t. MLS NEXT Academy Division Events. This definition includes, but is not limited to,
MLS NEXT Academy Division-sanctioned tournaments, leagues, showcases, Talent
IDs, and camps run by Operators and/or PDev, including local affiliate organizations (if
the event is sanctioned by Operator or PDev). While Operator or PDev may not have
authority or control over non-sanctioned events operated or organized by Clubs, any
conduct prohibited by the Rules and Policies even at non-sanctioned events may serve as
an independent basis for disciplinary action if it calls into question a Participant’s fitness
to participate in MLS NEXT Academy Division Events or Competitions.
u. MLS NEXT Academy Division Participation Agreement and Waiver. The waiver and
release agreement that all Players (and, to the extent applicable, Players’ parents/legal
guardians), Club Representatives, or any other Participant must sign before they may
participate in MLS NEXT Academy Division.
v. MLS NEXT Academy Division Regional Events: MLS NEXT Academy Division
Competitions held regionally by an Operator across select age groups. Unless otherwise
stated by Operator, Club participation is required at MLS NEXT Academy Division
Regional Events, which are part of the Regular Season.
w. MLS NEXT Academy Division Technical Standards. All rules and guidelines related to
the technical soccer standards of any MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition as
well as training standards. These include, but are not limited to, substitutions, field size,
and coach licensing and certification.
x. MLS NEXT Academy Division Website. The official MLS NEXT Academy Division
website.
y. MLS NEXT Cup. The MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition held at the
conclusion of the Regular Season among the qualifying Teams from the U15 through
U19 age groups, culminating in a champion in the U15, U16, U17, and U19 age groups.
z. Operator. The entity provided a license by PDev to host, operate, oversee, manage,
support, and be responsible for MLS NEXT Academy Division Conference in an
assigned territory.
aa. Participant. Any Player, Club, Club Representative, Operator employee that does not
otherwise fall under the definition of Program Staff, Match Official, parent, spectator,
volunteer, independent contractor (including, but not limited to, security or medical
personnel including an athletic trainer that does not fall under Club Representative), or
other individual that attends or contributes to an MLS NEXT Academy Division Event.
3

bb. Player. An individual who has completed the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Registration process and is eligible to be added to a Roster.
cc. Post-Season. The MLS NEXT Cup.
dd. Pre-Season. The three-to-four week period before the start of the MLS NEXT Academy
Division Regular Season.
ee. Program Staff. Those individuals who work on behalf of Operator to execute all MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division Events.
ff. Protest. An objection of any kind related to a Match Decision.
gg. Registration. The process by which each Player, Club, and Club Representative submits
required information and agrees to abide by the Rules and Policies. Registration is
currently done online.
hh. Regular Season. The time period beginning on a date set by Operator (typically in
September) and concluding on a date specified by Operator before the beginning of the
MLS NEXT Cup during which MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition between
Clubs occurs, inclusive of regional and winter MLS NEXT Academy Division Events,
and counting towards points for regular season standings. Excluded from the Regular
Season is any breaks for high school season in a given Conference.
ii. Roster. The list of Players and Club Representatives registered to participate on a Team.
jj. Rules and Policies. Collectively, these MLS NEXT Academy Division Rules and
Regulations, along with each Club’s Membership Agreement, the MLS NEXT Academy
Division Commercial Marketing Guidelines, the MLS NEXT Academy Division Style
Guide, the MLS NEXT Academy Division Disciplinary Code, the MLS NEXT Academy
Division Code of Conduct, the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing
Policy, the MLS Privacy Policy, and any other rules, regulations, policies, guidelines,
procedures, or directives of Operator and/or PDev, as they exist on the date hereof and
as they may be amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to
time).
kk. Sanction. A penalty or other means to enforce compliance with Rules and Policies.
ll. Season. The entire year of competitive and non-competitive programming organized by
Operator as MLS NEXT Academy Division Events, including, but not limited to, Pre-
Season, Regular Season, and Post-Season.
mm. Team. Any grouping of Players by age by a Club to play in MLS NEXT Academy
Division Competitions.
4

NN. USSF (the “Federation” or “U.S. Soccer”). The United States Soccer Federation, the
official governing body of the sport of soccer in the United States.
III. SAFESPORT, ANTI-ABUSE, ANTI-DISCRIMINATION, AND SAFETY
COMPLIANCE FRAMEWORK
The MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy, which incorporates policies
mandated by federal law, the U.S. Center for SafeSport (the “Center), as well as the U.S. Soccer
Federation, represents a foundational structure for building a soccer community where all
participants, be they athletes, coaches, referees, parents, administrators, or volunteers, play an
active role in creating a soccer atmosphere free of all forms of emotional, physical, and sexual
misconduct.
In addition, the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy prohibits engaging
in any conduct that amounts to unfair, adverse, discriminatory, or unequal treatment based on an
individual’s Protected Status, including, without limitation, racism, or sexism. Operators are
committed to maintaining an environment at all MLS NEXT Academy Division Events that is free
from all forms of Discrimination.
The MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy is incorporated into all Rules
and Policies and can be found on the MLS NEXT Academy Division Website. Failure of any
Participant to comply with the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy or the
MLS NEXT Academy Division Code of Conduct will subject the non-compliant Club and/or
Participant to discipline in accordance with the MLS NEXT Academy Division Disciplinary Code
and the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy.
IV. GOVERNANCE AND ORGANIZATION
a. Authority & Discretion
MLS NEXT Academy Division is managed and administered by Operators. By signing the MLS
NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement, Clubs agree that the decision-making and
policy-setting of Operator for the Conference (along with licensor PDev in limited circumstances),
shall be made in the sole and absolute discretion of Operator.
b. Discipline
Any and all Infractions may subject a Club or a Participant to discipline under the MLS NEXT
Academy Division Disciplinary Code. Should a Club or Participant be found in violation of any
of the Rules and Policies, penalties may vary based on the type and frequency of the Infraction
and may result in fines, denied participation in MLS NEXT Academy Division Competitions and
Events, expulsion from the Program, among other potential penalties in Operator’s sole and
absolute discretion (along with licensor PDev in limited circumstances). The Clubs may bear the
financial costs of any training mandated as a result of any rule violation. For more information,
please see the MLS NEXT Academy Division Disciplinary Code.
5

V. MLS NEXT ACADEMY DIVISION MEMBERSHIP
a. Consideration For Membership
Each Club that wishes to participate in MLS NEXT Academy Division must submit a completed
application prior to the application deadline determined by PDev to be considered for admission
into the Program. The precise criteria that will be applied in assessing applicant clubs will be
determined by PDev in its sole and absolute discretion. However, it is expected that the following
factors, among others, may be considered, with the weight of each factor to be decided by PDev
in its sole and absolute discretion:
i. Club philosophy
ii. Leadership and governance
iii. History of elite player development
iv. Strength of current and potential player pool
v. Expertise and credentials of coaches and technical staff
vi. Daily performance environment, including training, matches, facilities,
staff support, non-soccer programs, etc.
vii. Ability to reduce or eliminate costs for players
viii. Geography
ix. Disciplinary history (including criminal or Safety & Wellbeing violations)
x. Evidence of non-discriminatory, inclusive policies, processes and practices
xi. Participation and standing in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
PDev will aim to review and evaluate each application every calendar year. Once PDev has
reviewed the submitted applications, PDev will conduct live (virtual) interviews with clubs under
consideration and gather feedback from local and national references.
PDev will make the final determination regarding which clubs are accepted and offered the
opportunity to become Members of MLS NEXT Academy Division. An accepted Club will not
become a Member until the Club has agreed to abide by all MLS NEXT Academy Division Rules
and Policies, provided the required performance-bond payment, and entered into a fully-executed
MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement.
6

b. Maintaining Eligibility
To remain in good standing, Clubs must be in full compliance with the Rules and Policies. In
addition, Operator reserves the right to conduct evaluations of Clubs at any time; such evaluations
may include review of both technical and administrative criteria (including as set forth above in
Section V(a)), as determined by Operator in its sole discretion, as well as a review of the Club’s
compliance with the Rules and Policies.
c. Change of Ownership
A Club must give Operator and PDev written notice if the Club wants to change ownership, merge
with another Club, change the location of the Club, or change the structure of the Club. PDev
reserves the right to deny the Club’s request, conduct a review of the Club prior to any such change,
or determine whether the Club will remain in good standing. If Operator determines that a Club is
not in compliance with such criteria or any of the Rules and Policies, the Club may be subject to
discipline, up to and including removal from MLS NEXT Academy Division.
d. Withdrawal of a Club
In the event a Club withdraws from MLS NEXT Academy Division without finishing its scheduled
Season, the Club shall forfeit its Membership and performance bond, and the following will apply:
i. If at least half of the full schedule of Matches for the Club in question has
been played, all resulting scores stand as validated by Operator;
ii. If less than half of the full schedule of Matches for the Club in question has
been played, these completed Matches will be deleted from the standings;
and
iii. In the sole and absolute discretion of Operator, any remaining Matches shall
either be recorded as a Forfeit victory for the opponent and/or rescheduled,
as may be appropriate.
e. Termination
Pursuant to the MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement, Operator reserves the
right to terminate any Club’s Membership at any time for failure to meet MLS NEXT Academy
Division Technical Standards, violation of any of the Rules and Policies, or taking or failing to
stop any action that frustrates the purpose or intent of any Rules and Policies, in each case such
determination shall be in accordance with the MLS NEXT Academy Division Disciplinary Code.
VI. CLUB NON-FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
a. Compliance with and Policies
All Clubs and Participants are required to comply with the Rules and Policies. In order to comply,
Clubs must adopt the Rules and Policies on behalf of their employees and all other Club
7

Representatives. Operator reserves the right to decide how to enforce the Rules and Policies, in its
sole and absolute discretion. Should a Club or any Participant associated with that Club be found
in violation of any of the DIVISION Rules and Policies, the Club’s standing and Membership in
MLS NEXT Academy Division, or the individual’s participation in MLS NEXT Academy
Division, may be jeopardized, as further described below. In addition, the Rules and Policies are
subject to change from time to time. Although Operator and PDev make every effort not to modify
the Rules and Policies mid-season, Operator and PDev reserve the right, in their sole and absolute
discretion, to amend or enact new policies at any time, particularly to the extent required by
applicable law, recommended by the U.S. Olympic and Paralympic Committee or the Center, or
otherwise where they believe it is in the best interest of MLS NEXT Academy Division and/or the
safety of its Players. In the event of a change of these Rules and Policies, Operator will make best
efforts to provide Clubs with advance notice.
Subject to any applicable laws, Operator may use any information in determining disqualifications
for participation in MLS NEXT Academy Division. Such information may include, without
limitation, conviction of any felony, gross misdemeanors, violation of Rules and Policies, or
driving offenses, including, but not limited to: loss of license, reckless driving, impaired driving,
or a DUI.
Any person accused of physical or sexual misconduct or otherwise violating the MLS NEXT
Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy may be terminated, suspended, or placed in an
alternate position by Operator until the matter is resolved. A person who is accused, but later
cleared, of charges/allegations may apply to be reinstated in MLS NEXT Academy Division.
Reinstatement is not a right, and no guarantee is made that they will be reinstated to their former
position or any other position within the MLS NEXT ecosystem.
b. Reporting Obligations
All Clubs and Covered Persons (as defined in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and
Wellbeing Policy to mean any individual age eighteen or older (“Adult”) that is a Participant who
has Regular Contact (which means, recurring, repeated or periodic contact between an Adult and
anyone under the lesser of (1) the age of 18, or (2) the age of majority in the applicable state or
province (“Minor”), or a person with supervisory or decision-making authority over an Adult who
has recurring, repeated or periodic contact with Minors, including but not limited to overseeing,
supervising, chaperoning, or otherwise interacting) with any Player or Match Official, who is a
Minor, in connection with Covered Programs (as defined in the Safety and Wellbeing Policy))
must comply with all reporting obligations set forth in the Rules and Policies. Failure by Clubs or
Covered Persons to promptly report violations of the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and
Wellbeing Policy and SafeSport Code constitutes a violation of the Rules and Policies. Clubs,
Covered Persons, and all Participants should report pertinent situations that appear to be in non-
compliance with the Rules and Policies to Operator. Reporting non-compliance on the part of other
Clubs should not be viewed as a negative, but rather as a positive attempt to maintain consistent
standards that ensure professionalism, safety, and fairness, both on and off the field.
8

c. Conduct
Each Club must, upon Operator’s renewal of a Club’s membership in MLS NEXT Academy
Division, execute and deliver its annual Membership Agreement to Operator prior to the start of
the upcoming season. In the event a Club does not renew its Membership by executing the annual
Membership Agreement and paying the performance bond as directed by Operator, such Club will
not be permitted to participate in MLS NEXT Academy Division Events for that year. In the event
a Club does not provide adequate notice (at least thirty (30) days) of such non-renewal, the Club
will forfeit its performance bond. Each Club shall be responsible for the conduct of all its
Participants, including, but not limited to, its Club Representatives, Players, parents, volunteers,
and spectators.
d. Development
i. Heading:
1. Players aged 10 and younger are prohibited from heading the ball
regardless of the age group in which they play.
2. Players who are 11, 12, and 13, regardless of the age group in which
they play, may head the ball during Matches; however, these players
must limit heading practice to a maximum of 30 minutes per week
with no more than 15–20 headers per player, per week.
e. Scholarships
i. Requirements:
1. Each Club in MLS NEXT Academy Division is required to issue a
minimum of one (1) full scholarship equivalent per season.
2. The full scholarship equivalent must be a financial amount equal to
the cost of participating on an MLS NEXT Academy Division team,
excluding travel (i.e., cost of tuition).
a. The financial support that results in a full scholarship
equivalent may be issued across multiple families (e.g.,
instead of providing a single player/family one (1) full
scholarship, a Club may issue financial assistance to four (4)
families that is equal to 25% of a full scholarship).
ii. Recommendations (not required):
1. Club financial support to families is “need-based.”
2. Clubs issue financial support across several age groups.
9

VII. OUTSIDE COMPETITION
a. Permissible Outside Competition
Subject to approval by Operator (and in the event of international tournament participation,
additional USSF approvals may be required), Clubs may participate in tournaments and
competitions outside of the MLS NEXT Academy Division Program. Clubs will only be permitted
to participate in approved competitions and events that follow the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Technical Standards and are in compliance with the standards outlined in the MLS NEXT
Academy Division Outside Competition Standards. A list of previously approved sanctioned
tournaments are included below. Clubs must receive prior approval from Operator for all
sanctioned tournaments outside of the MLS NEXT Academy Division Program each season. Club
participation in outside competition without prior approval may result in discipline.
To request permission, the Club must submit to Operator an MLS NEXT Academy Division
Outside Competition Application, including a roster and all necessary background information.
All applications will be reviewed within 72 hours of submission; however, if additional
information is required, allow 14 business days for review. In the event the Team plans to travel
internationally, the Club must also submit the appropriate documentation to the USSF (if the Club
is based in the U.S.). Clubs will ensure that they receive required permission and secure necessary
participant excess medical coverage.
The MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy and the prevention policies
therein remain applicable to any Club traveling internationally.
b. Player Participation in Outside Competitions
Players must meet all attendance and participation requirements under the Rules and Policies and
under any individual Club requirements with respect to training and Matches for their Club during
the Season. Absent extenuating circumstances (to be determined by Operator and the Club), the
failure to meet those requirements should result in the Player’s removal from the Club. Operator
strongly encourages Clubs to ensure that Players meet the attendance and participation
requirements—Clubs that do not take appropriate action to ensure that such attendance and
participation requirements are met may be subject to Sanctions.
Clubs may not permit any Player to participate in outside competition with another Club during
the Season, except as set forth in this Section and in connection with a Player’s National Team
duty and/or Youth National Team Identification Center participation. Clubs will be held
accountable for the actions of their Players and Club Representatives, as well as the Club’s parents,
spectators, and volunteers. Players will be subject to discipline by both their own Club and
Operator.
10

VIII. EXPECTATIONS OF CLUBS
a. Training and Match Expectations
All Clubs are required to complete their entire Regular Season schedule and to attend all MLS
NEXT Academy Division Events scheduled for their age groups. As further detailed in the MLS
NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement, failure to field a Team for a scheduled Match
resulting in a Forfeit will cause the Club to be fined. All Forfeits are subject to additional fines and
disciplinary action at Operator’s sole and absolute discretion. Any Club forfeiting a Match due to
failing to field a Team during its membership in MLS NEXT Academy Division may be removed
from the Program in PDev’s sole and absolute discretion.
Clubs are responsible for fielding teams that provide meaningful competition for other Members.
In the event a Club is not able to field consistently competitive teams in MLS NEXT Academy
Division, Operator will notify the Club of the concern and work in good faith on solutions and
timelines to improve the Club’s competitiveness. In the event the Club is unable to become more
competitive, such that the Club is providing meaningful Matches for other Members, the Club may
be subject to removal from the Program.
Clubs are responsible for adhering to all Match Day policies and procedures outlined and explained
in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Technical Standards. Clubs should make efforts to acquire
the best facilities possible as outlined below. Clubs must train and play home Matches within
reasonable geographic proximity to their Club’s home location unless approved by Operator. If a
Club is unable to provide a home field that meets MLS NEXT Academy Division minimum
standards (70 x 105 yards for U15 and older; 65 x 100 yards for U14; and 64 x 84 yards for U13),
the Club may be asked to relocate to an alternate location or may lose the right to host Matches.
b. Inclusion and Non-Discrimination
MLS NEXT Academy Division is an inclusive and non-discriminatory Program. MLS NEXT
Academy Division does not and will not tolerate any racist, discriminatory or inappropriately
exclusive behavior by any Participant, including any Club.
MLS NEXT Academy Division has set out to create an inclusive development environment. To
that end, Clubs shall make all efforts to establish a positive and safe environment for all
Participants, without regard to race, ethnicity, income level, culture, sexual orientation, and gender.
As outlined in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy, it is recommended
that each Club provide inclusion and awareness training for its Participants. To promote a positive
and inclusive culture, Clubs should use best efforts to provide training on an annual basis and
ensure that their Players and Club Representatives complete such training prior to the beginning
of the Regular Season (and more frequently if needed).
c. Non-Technical Facility Requirements
Clubs should acquire Facilities that (if feasible): (1) have separate locker rooms for Players on
different teams; (2) have separate and functionally equivalent changing areas for different genders,
as well as separate restrooms for Adults and Minors; and (3) the field must be designed for soccer
11

use and adequately maintained and repaired. In addition, prior to the use of a field, the field must
be inspected as set forth in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy.
d. Mandatory Meeting Attendance
The Club must send at least one (1) Club director and one (1) Club administrator to each mandatory
MLS NEXT Academy Division meeting or conference call as scheduled by Operator, unless
otherwise notified.
e. Recruiting Players/Territories
No Club has exclusive territory rights. Clubs may recruit Players for participation on Clubs from
any geographic region. Players cannot move back and forth between Clubs more than once in a
given Season.
IX. CLUB FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
a. Performance Bond
As further described in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement, each Club
will be required to post a performance bond of $2,000 prior to being declared an official Member.
In the event PDev does not approve the Club for renewed membership, Operator will return the
Club’s performance bond. In the event a Club is removed from MLS NEXT Academy Division
for any reason outlined in the Rules and Policies, the performance bond shall be forfeited by the
Club and the Club shall have no further rights regarding the performance bond.
b. Travel Costs
All costs associated with travel to MLS NEXT Academy Division Competitions are the
responsibility of the individual Club.
c. Training/Coaching Costs
All costs associated with the training and coaching of a Club are the responsibility of the individual
Club.
d. Administrative Costs
All costs associated with running a Club, including, without limitation, all insurance premiums
deductibles, and/or self insured retentions required by the Rules and Policies or otherwise, are the
responsibility of the individual Club.
e. Registration Costs
All Players and Club Representatives who will be allowed on any Team’s technical area during an
Match must register with Operator to participate in MLS NEXT Academy Division. As detailed
in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement, registration fees for the upcoming
12

season shall be $60 per participating individual. Coaches and Club Representatives do not need to
pay more if coaching across different age groups or serving in multiple roles, each individual only
needs to be accounted for once. Fees are subject to change upon reasonable notice to Clubs. Clubs
must pay registration fees for all players and staff members before Operator will approve them for
participation in MLS NEXT Academy Division.
f. Team Registration Costs
As detailed in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement, team registration fees
for the upcoming season shall be $700 per participating team.
g. Regional Event Costs
The registration cost to attend the mandatory MLS Next Academy Division Regional Event as
scheduled by Operator will be $1,400 per participating team.
h. Background Check Costs
The Club will cover all costs associated with the background checks required in the Rules and
Policies and conducted through the Registration system by Operator’s selected vendor.
i. ImPACT Baseline Tests Costs
The Club will cover all costs associated with administration and delivery of individual ImPACT
Baseline Concussion testing required in the Rules and Policies.
j. Equipment Costs
Other than equipment that Operator elects to provide in its sole discretion, all costs associated with
equipment are the responsibility of the individual Club.
k. Facility Costs
All costs associated with securing a Facility for training and home Matches are the responsibility
of the individual Club. The host Club is responsible for covering Facility parking fees (if
applicable) for the Match Officials, visiting Club Representatives, and Team transportation (e.g.,
bus/van) for Matches.
l. Match Officials Fees
The home Team will cover referee, assistant referee, and assignor fees for all age groups in
Matches during the Regular Season that are scheduled by Operator. The home and visiting Teams
will split previously stated referee fees for all age groups at Matches that are not part of the Regular
Season, including the MLS NEXT Academy Division Showcases, MLS NEXT Academy Division
Regional Events, MLS NEXT Cup, and any other MLS NEXT Academy Division event requiring
match officials. Operator reserves the right to assess sanctions for outstanding Match Official Fees.
13

m. Fines/Penalties
Operator reserves the right to assess fines and/or sanctions for Infractions, as outlined in the MLS
NEXT Academy Division Disciplinary Code. All of the money collected in fines will be provided
to the MLS GO PLAY FUND, Major League Soccer’s initiative to make soccer more accessible
for all children.
n. Certificate of Insurance
Each Club shall carry and maintain liability insurance and, upon request by Operator and at Club’s
insurance renewals, shall provide Operator with certificate(s) of insurance evidencing the Club’s
insurance coverage in the amounts and for the coverages required by Operator and the Rules and
Policies. For further information, see the MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement.
o. Other
Unless otherwise expressly addressed in these regulations, all expenses related to a Club and the
Club’s participation in MLS NEXT Academy Division are the sole responsibility of the
participating Club.
X. SECONDARY PLAYER MEDICAL INSURANCE
Operator provides secondary medical accident insurance for each Player. This medical accident
insurance is secondary to (i.e., excess over) the Player’s personal health insurance and only covers
injuries occurring during MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition, MLS NEXT Academy
Division training, MLS NEXT Academy Division tryouts, and approved Outside Competition. For
the avoidance of doubt, this medical insurance does not cover Coaches, other staff, or spectators.
Further, Operator will not cover health insurance for Players that drop their primary health
insurance after joining Operator for the purpose of making this insurance the Player’s primary
medical insurance policy. Sublimits, deductibles, coinsurance, and other conditions may apply.
XI. REGISTRATION
a. General
Operator will collect the personal information of all Players and certain Club Representatives via
online registration system software, to the extent such information is necessary to enable Operator
to provide services (e.g., match execution).
Operator shall have the authority to establish additional procedures, including deadlines, for
Registration of Players through the registration software.
b. Club and Club Representative Registration
i. Club Registration
14

All Clubs must register through the online Registration process via the registration platform
designated by Operator, which shall include the following:
• Registration of Club in Operator’s designated soccer registration software; and
• Paying a $60 registration fee for each individual that will be on the field or sideline for a
Club during a Match.
• Paying a $700 registration fee for each team that will be participating in MLS NEXT
Academy Division.
• Individuals who have not cleared the background check process or received approval from
Operator or its designated third-party vendor cannot participate in any MLS NEXT
Academy Division activity.
• Any Club that allows an unapproved/unregistered individual to participate in any MLS
NEXT Academy Division activity may be subject to sanctions including, but not limited
to, a fine or termination of its MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership, and will be
considered in breach of its MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement.
ii. Club Representative Registration
All Club Representatives seeking to work with any Player, including, without limitation, any
Covered Person as defined in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy,
registered Health Care Professionals (“HCP”), Certified Athletic Trainers (“ATC”) at any practice,
Match, or other MLS NEXT Academy Division Event must be fully registered. Names and photos
are created digitally through the MLS NEXT Academy Division registration software and will be
used to check in Club Representatives who will be in the Club’s technical area via the Match Day
Roster. A maximum of five (5) Coaches/staff members (not including Qualified Medical
Professional (“QMP”) or other HCP/ATC, video analyst, videographer, Club Sporting Director,
or Club General Manager) from a Club may be in the technical area during a Match and all must
have a photo within the Match Day Roster.
To request approval, all Club Representatives must complete the online Registration process
through Operator’s platform by:
• Completing the Operator-mandated background check (and for new hires a reference check
conducted by the Club as well);
• Uploading a photo of themselves;
• Completing the U.S. Center for SafeSport’s Core Training(“Core Training”) (for initial
approval) or, if the Core training has already been completed, the official SafeSport
refresher course for each subsequent season (available in your training account at
www.safesport.org);
• Acknowledge all provided Rules and Policies;
15

• Completing the CDC HEADS UP concussion training; and
• Submitting a signed MLS NEXT Academy Division Participant Agreement and Waiver
for the applicable season.
NOTE: Individuals subject to disciplinary sanction by the Center or Operator, whether a temporary
or final measure, may not be permitted to participate in MLS NEXT Academy Division. Clubs
must consult the on-line disciplinary records at www.safesport.org
(https://uscenterforsafesport.org/response-and-resolution/centralized-disciplinary-database/) as
part of their employment background screening process.
All Coaches and Club directors are required to complete annual equity and inclusion training, as
directed by Operator.
c. Player Registration
All individuals seeking to play for a must be fully registered. Names and photos are created
digitally through the MLS NEXT Academy Division registration software and will be used to
check in Players who will be in the Club’s technical area via the Match Day Roster.
All Players must complete the online registration process through Operator via the designated
registration platform, including the following:
i. Execute the MLS NEXT Academy Division Participant Agreement and
Waiver and the Player Authorization for the Release of Health Information;
ii. Upload a photo;
iii. Complete an ImPACT Baseline Test;
iv. Provide medical insurance information;
v. Provide required medical clearance information;
vi. Acknowledge and agree to comply with the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Code of Conduct, MLS Privacy Policy, the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Rules and Regulations, and MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety &
Wellbeing Policy;
vii. Complete all aspects of the MLS NEXT Academy Division Registration
process and affirm you are not subject to suspension; and
viii. Complete mandated SafeSport training, if applicable.
16

d. Compliance with Registration Process
i. MLS NEXT Academy Division may disapprove, deny registration to,
and/or disqualify any individual, and/or deny Membership to or otherwise
discipline any Club, which employs or utilizes any individual who either
fails to comply with the registration procedures, falsifies information,
violates the Rules and Policies, or as a result of such procedures is
determined by MLS NEXT Academy Division to be unfit to continue in
their current position.
ii. Anyone who forges a document or falsifies an authentic document or uses
a forged or falsified document to deceive may be Sanctioned.
XII. PLAYER ELIGIBILITY AND ROSTERING
a. Eligible Players
To be considered an eligible Player, any Player listed on any Club Roster must meet the age
requirement for their Club, have completed the registration process outlined in Section XI(c), have
paid the registration fee, and must not be subject to a Suspension (to serve a suspension, a Player
is required to sit for the entire subsequent match day; the Player is not eligible to play with an older
age group on the day the Player serves the suspension).
Players can only be approved and made eligible by Operator.
Players not listed on a Club’s Roster or any Player listed on a Club’s Roster but not eligible to
play, due to reasons such as missing MLS NEXT Academy Division registration forms, awaiting
international clearance, serving a suspension, or not being listed on the official Match Day Roster
and line-up sheet, shall be considered an ineligible Player. A Club using an ineligible Player during
an MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition may be subject to a Sanction as determined by
MLS NEXT Academy Division and/or a Forfeit (by a 0-3 margin) of any Matches in which the
ineligible Player participated.
Each participating Club shall ensure the following when selecting its Players for each age group
in an MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition:
i. All Players remain subject to Operator’s jurisdiction;
ii. All Players shall be eligible for participation in accordance with the relevant
MLS NEXT Academy Division and FIFA regulations; and
iii. All Players shall meet the applicable age requirements:
17

Age Group Born on or After
U13 January 1, 2013
U14 January 1, 2012
U15 January 1, 2011
U16 January 1, 2010
U17 January 1, 2009
U19 January 1, 2007
b. MLS NEXT HOMEGROWN DIVISION Club Roster
i. Match Day Rosters:
Each Team must create and submit via a digital form a Match Day Roster to the referee prior to a
Match. Each Match Day Roster must include all Players and Match Day Club Representatives.
Clubs are responsible for verifying the accuracy of Match Day Rosters and signing a completed
copy from the Match Officials after the match. Operator will update the MLS NEXT Academy
Division Rosters weekly on the MLS NEXT Academy Division Registration website to reflect any
suspensions, additions or drops, or other eligibility issues.
ii. General:
The date by which each Club must provide an initial MLS NEXT Academy Division Roster for
the 2025–2026 season is August 18, 2025 Each Club’s Roster consists of a maximum of twenty-
eight (28) Players with a minimum of 14 Players. There is no limit to the number of Rostered
Players that do not have United States citizenship.
iii. Injured Players:
If a Club is operating at the Roster maximum, it may replace a Player with a serious injury (as
supported by written documentation from a medical professional detailing the injury) with a new
Player in their Roster spot. If the injured Player is medically cleared to return during the Season,
the Club may allow him/her/them to replace the Player that took his/her/their Roster spot..
iv. Match Roster Size:
For U13 through U19 Matches, only twenty (20) Players from the Roster will be eligible to
participate in each Match, and these Players must be placed on the Match Day Roster (showing
Players that will start and Players eligible for substitution).
v. Bench and Technical Area:
18

The bench and technical area are reserved for Players and Club Representatives listed on the
Team’s Match Day Roster (a maximum of twenty (20) Players, including nine (9) subs on bench).
In addition, a maximum of five (5) registered Club Representatives may sit in the technical area,
not including a QMP or other registered HCP or ATC. Any person in the technical area during a
Match must be registered as a Player or Club Representative and listed on the Match Day Roster,
other than a QMP or other HCP or ATC that is not employed by the Club. Any person in the
technical area during a Match that is not registered as a Player or Club Representative will be
subject to sanctions.
c. Younger Players Playing Up
Players on the U13, U14, U15, U16 and U17 MLS NEXT Academy Division Teams may be
selected for the U14, U15, U16, U17 and U19 Match Day Rosters, respectively. Players playing
up can only compete in cumulative minutes equaling one full match (i.e., 90 minutes for U17 and
U19 Players) per day split across two (2) Matches.
d. International Clearance
Per FIFA’s Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players, as it may be amended or restated
from time to time, the international clearance process is required when any foreign-born Player
over the age of 10 attempts to register with an affiliated club in the United States, regardless of
that Player’s ability or citizenship.1 FIFA regulations require Operator to obtain appropriate
clearances on behalf of these Players before they can register and participate in MLS NEXT
Academy Division.
1 According to FIFA, these Regulations serve an important function in protecting minors: “Young footballers are
vulnerable to potential exploitation and abuse when they are in a foreign country without proper controls. While
international transfers might, in specific cases, be favourable to a young player’s sporting career, they are likely
to be contrary to the best interests of the vast majority of players as minors. For FIFA, protecting the appropriate
and stable development of a minor as a whole should prevail over purely sporting interests. In the past, the
trafficking of young players to clubs, mainly in Europe, by unscrupulous persons led to some minors, whose talent
may not have met the expectations of the respective clubs, being virtually abandoned on the streets in foreign
countries. In these cases, minor players often do not know the language or culture and have no means to return to
their home countries. It is also necessary to mention that, in some cases, what is questioned is not necessarily the
quality of the training and overall education provided to the minors by this or that club, but the methods used to
bring the minors into the club. Today, although FIFA’s approach may appear harsh in an individual case, it is
only by enforcing the rules consistently and strictly that the abuses of the past can be avoided, and a proper and
safe development of minor players secured.”
19

XIII. COMPETITION
a. Overview
Operator will place Clubs into geographically-based Conferences and set a schedule. Operator
reserves the right to adjust Match scheduling.
b. Schedule
Operator manages the MLS NEXT Academy Division competition schedule, inclusive of the Post-
Season. MLS NEXT Cup typically will be held at the end of the Regular Season. If a Club would
like to change a Fixture date, it must contact Operator four (4) weeks in advance of the Fixture
date. All changes are subject to the approval of Operator.
c. Venue/Kick-off Times/Turnaround Time
Operator shall set the dates of all MLS NEXT Academy Division Competitions. The venues and
specific kick-off times of the Matches shall be proposed by the home Team, subject to the approval
of. The home Team shall submit venues and specific kick-off times to Operator for all Matches by
a date determined each year by Operator, so visiting Teams can make the appropriate arrangements
or offer objections for Operator’s consideration. All Clubs must provide a turf or synthetic
alternative in the event of inclement weather. Evening Matches may only be played at venues with
appropriate lighting installations.
i. Double Fixture weekends may be played; however, a minimum of eighteen
(18) hours must be provided between both kick-off times to allow for
necessary travel and recovery.
1. For instance, if a Saturday Fixture kick-off is 6:00 pm local time,
the Sunday Fixture may not kick-off earlier than 12:00 pm local time
unless authorized by Operator.
ii. Clubs may not request to schedule three (3) Matches in four (4) days during
Regular Season competition.
iii. Matches may not kick off earlier than 9:00 am local time without permission
from Program Staff.
iv. When scheduling home Matches, Clubs should adhere to the following
guidelines with regard to kick-off times and order of Matches:
1. The home Team determines kick-off order.
2. Operator recommends that Matches be played from oldest to
youngest when possible.
20

3. When scheduling multiple Matches on the same field, please allow
for at least thirty (30) minutes of warm-up time prior to each kickoff.
4. Less than thirty (30) minutes between each match is permitted if
there is adequate warm-up space on an adjacent field.
v. Moving a Fixture venue or kick-off time for any reason within fourteen (14)
days of the scheduled Fixture may result in fines and/or Sanctions that will
be levied in accordance with the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Disciplinary Code.
d. Facility Set-Up
The home Team shall be responsible for setting up its venue to include the appropriate lines, goals,
nets, corner flags, benches, and water set up for both Teams and ensuring a clean and well-
maintained field in accordance with the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing
Policy. The home Team will also provide the official adidas match balls.
e. Abandonment
If a Club refuses to play a Match or to continue playing one that has begun, and there is no rationale
under the Rules and Policies detailing why this action would be permissible, it may be Sanctioned
by forfeiting the Match. Additional Sanctions may be imposed against the Club in Operator’s sole
discretion. In serious cases, the Club may also be disqualified from the MLS NEXT Academy
Division Competition in progress and its MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership may be
revoked.
f. Postponement
All postponements and cancellations for any reason other than the health and safety of Participants
must be approved by Operator. A Club may postpone any Match in the event the Club believes in
good faith that proceeding with a Match would pose a significant risk to the health and safety of
any Participants.
g. Rescheduling a Postponed Match
Provided the postponement is approved by Operator or is made in accordance with the exceptions
provided for herein, the Clubs in question are given the first opportunity to arrange a reschedule
date for a postponed Match. If there is no rescheduled date within seven (7) days after the
postponed Fixture, Operator will choose the date, location, and time for the rescheduled Match
and each Club will be required to participate, subject to any health and safety guidelines released
by Operator. Fees associated with that date will be the responsibility of the involved Club(s).
h. Rescheduling Considerations
U13 Matches must reach the 50th minute to be considered a completed Match. U14 Matches must
reach the 60th minute to be considered a completed Match. U15, U16, U17, and U19 Matches must
21

reach the 75th minute to be considered a completed Match. If a Match does not reach the designated
threshold, Operator at its sole discretion shall have the authority to review all of the facts (including
fault on the part of either Team, unavoidable conditions, expense for both Teams, and requests by
a Team to take a particular action in the best interests of MLS NEXT Academy Division, the Clubs,
and the Club’s teams) in determining whether and when a Match should be rescheduled, who
should bear the financial burden as a result of cancellation or rescheduling, whether the Match
should be forfeited or cancelled permanently, or whether other fines should be levied. Unless the
Sanction or remedy is set forth specifically in any Rules and Policies, Operator shall decide what
action to take, and Operator’s decision shall be final (subject to the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Disciplinary Code).
i. Schedule Completion
Every Team must play every Match on its schedule. Teams that fail to play every Match may face
a Forfeit or may be subject to a fine. Further, Operator at its sole discretion may exclude any
Club/Team from Post-Season participation for failure to play every scheduled Match.
j. Facility Clearances
Once a Club has submitted its venues and kick-off times, Operator assumes that a Team’s home
Facility is available for all dates on its schedule. If the home Facility is not available, an alternative
location must be provided by the home Team. For the avoidance of doubt, all facilities remain
subject to approval by Operator and must comply with all Rules and Policies. Operator reserves
the right to reject approval for any facility at Operator’s sole and reasonable discretion.
k. Standings
Operator assumes the responsibility of posting the official MLS NEXT Academy Division league
standings on the MLS NEXT Academy Division Website for the U15 – U19 ages. The official
MLS NEXT Academy Division league standings include wins, losses, ties, goals scored for, goals
scored against, points per match, and total league points.
Result Points
Win 3
Tie 1
Loss 0
Forfeit 0–3 loss
All standings are generated by the Match Report submitted online by the referee within twenty-
four (24) hours of the completion of the Match. Teams have forty-eight (48) hours to verify their
Match Results with Operator.
22

l. Conference Tiebreakers
If two (2) or more Clubs finish the Regular Season with an equal number of Points per Match the
following procedure (in order of priority) will be used to break the tie and determine the final
standings (“MLS NEXT ACADEMY DIVISION Regular Season Tiebreaker”):
i. Head-to-head (only applicable to tie of two (2) Clubs).
ii. Number of Wins per Match.
iii. Goal differential per Match.
iv. Goals for per Match.
v. Away goal differential per Match
vi. Away goals for per Match
vii. Home goal differential per Match
viii. Home goals for per Match
ix. Fewest Disciplinary points:
1. One (1) point will be assessed for every Yellow Card received
during the competition.
2. Three (3) points will be assessed for every Red Card received during
the competition.
3. Five (5) points will be assessed for any ejections of staff or players
from the technical area during the competition.
x. Coin toss (tie of two (2) Clubs) or drawing of lots (tie of three (3) or more
Clubs).
After the winner of the Regular Season Tiebreaker advances, any remaining tied Clubs who did
not advance from the first round Regular Season Tiebreaker shall go through a second round
Regular Season Tiebreaker in order to determine the next advancing Club. The DIVISION Regular
Season Tiebreaker system will continue until qualification/seeding has been determined.
23

XIV. MATCHES, LAWS OF THE GAME, DISCIPLINARY MATTERS,
DISPUTES, PROTESTS, AND APPEALS
a. Laws of the Game
Unless otherwise provided in the Rules and Policies, all Matches shall be played in accordance
with the Laws of the Game in force at the time of the MLS NEXT Academy Division Competition
and as promulgated by the International Football Association Board, which can be found here.2 In
the case of any discrepancy in the interpretation of the Laws of the Game, the English version shall
be authoritative.
b. Disciplinary Matters
• Incidents and events requiring disciplinary action are handled in compliance with the
MLS NEXT Academy Division Disciplinary Code. The Players and Clubs participating
in MLS NEXT Academy Division agree to comply with the Laws of the Game (as
described above) and all Rules and Policies. The Players, Coaches, Club Representatives,
and Clubs agree to comply with all further directives set forth by Operator from time to
time, including, without limitation, any health and safety requirements or protocols.
• Any Coach/Club Representative who has received a Match Suspension cannot be present
at the Club facilities (including the soccer fields or Club offices) and cannot attend any
Club approved activity including, but not limited to, a Match, MLS NEXT Academy
Division Tournament, or other MLS NEXT Academy Division Event during the
pendency of the Sanction. Any suspended Coach/Club Representative who is found to
have violated this prohibition will receive an additional Match Suspension of two (2)
Fixtures.
• Reporting Egregious Fouls After the Fact
a. Clubs have 72 hours following a Match to report any egregious in-match fouls, not
acknowledged by the referee, to Operator after noticing the egregious foul during film
review.
c. Protests of Officiating Decisions
If a Match Decision is subject to Protest, it shall be submitted to the jurisdiction of Operator.
i. Time: Unless otherwise stipulated in this article, Protests shall be submitted
in writing to Operator within 12 hours of the conclusion of the Match in
question and followed up immediately with a full written report, including
a copy of the original Protest, to be sent in writing to Operator; otherwise,
they will be disregarded.
2 https://www.theifab.com/downloads/laws-of-the-game-2025-26?l=en
24

ii. Eligibility: Protests regarding the eligibility of Matches shall be submitted
in writing to Operator no later than 24 hours after the Match.
iii. Field Conditions: Protests regarding the state of the field, its surroundings,
markings, or accessory items (e.g., goals, flag posts, or soccer balls) shall
be made to the referee before the start of the Match by the Coach or
administrator lodging the Protest and should be included in the referee’s
post-Match Report. Operator must be notified in writing of the Protest prior
to kick-off. If the field’s playing surface becomes unplayable during a
Match, the referee shall consult Operator and both parties shall have the
joint right to determine whether to delay, postpone, or cancel the Match in
compliance with the MLS NEXT Academy Division Safety and Wellbeing
Policy.
iv. After a Match: Protests against any incidents that occur during the course
of a Match shall be made to Operator immediately following the completion
of the Match.
v. Referee’s Decisions: No Protests may be made about the referee’s decisions
regarding facts connected with play; such decisions are final.
vi. Frivolous Protests: If an unfounded or irresponsible Protest is lodged,
Operator may dismiss the Protest.
vii. For the avoidance of doubt, all violations of Rules and Policies other than
Match Decisions remain subject to the MLS NEXT Academy Division
Disciplinary Code.
d. Match Integrity Policy
MLS NEXT Academy Division prohibits match manipulation of any kind. Clubs that condone or
contemplate match manipulation will be subject to immediate expulsion from MLS NEXT
Academy Division and may face additional Sanctions.
Operator applies the FIFA Code of Ethics and prohibits involvement in soccer sports betting by
any Club Representatives or Players, regardless of whether sports betting may be legal in such
jurisdiction.
e. Disputes
Any Participant, Club, Club Representative, or individual or entity affiliated with any Club that is
involved in a dispute with Operator or a Club must first make a good faith effort to resolve such
dispute through negotiation before taking any other action. All disputes arising out of or related to
the MLS NEXT Academy Division Membership Agreement or the MLS NEXT Academy
Division Participant Agreement and Waiver shall be resolved by arbitration in accordance with the
terms in those agreements.
25

XV. COMMERCIAL RIGHTS AND EQUIPMENT
All Participants acknowledge that MLS is the owner of all marks and that use of said marks is
limited to what is expressly permitted and limited to the term during which the particular
Participant is affiliated with a Member or is itself a Club.
a. Uniforms
i. Players, Coaches, and Club Representatives are not allowed to display any
image or writing in any language or form on their playing or Team
uniforms, equipment (including bags, beverage containers, medical bags,
etc.), or body for the duration of official Matches, except for inoffensive
Team names and logos, sponsorships, and Player names, which are all
subject to review by Operator and PDev in advance of being displayed
during Matches. Players, Coaches, and Club Representatives with
offensive, obscene, distracting, or age -inappropriate tattoos may be asked
to cover them for the duration of a Match. Clubs are required to have one
white (or similarly light-colored) uniform and one dark uniform. Home
Teams will always wear white/light -color uniforms and visiting Teams will
be required to wear their dark/contrasting uniforms on single-Fixture
weekends. Clubs are required to apply the MLS NEXT Academy Division
uniform patch on the left sleeve of their match uniform. In the event the
Club cannot reasonably apply the patch on the left sleeve, the Club must
contact Operator for guidance on an alternate location. In the event Clubs
are not provided an MLS NEXT Academy Division patch for the beginning
of the 2025–2026 Season, Clubs must cover up the U.S. Soccer
Development Academy (or other national league) patch on their match
uniform. The Club is responsible for any costs associated with the MLS
NEXT Academy Division uniform patches and costs associated with the
application of its patch to its uniforms.
b. Soccer Balls
i. All Matches are to be played with the official Match ball. No Match can be
played without properly inflated Match balls. There shall be a minimum of
three (3) Match balls at each match which shall be inspected and subject to
approval by the referee. The U13 age group will play with size 4 balls. All
other age groups will play with size 5 balls, filled to 9.0–10.5 psi.
c. Commercial Rights
Clubs shall have the right to commercialize certain items in connection with their MLS NEXT
Academy Division participation, as outlined in the MLS NEXT Academy Division Commercial
Marketing Guidelines. Any Club that wishes to obtain sponsors in connection with its Club must
ensure that all such sponsorships comply with the MLS NEXT Academy Division Commercial
Marketing Guidelines. All decisions regarding the MLS NEXT Academy Division Commercial
26

Marketing Guidelines and any interpretation of the provisions of the guidelines shall be at the sole
and absolute discretion of PDev.
XVI. REFEREE AND MATCH INFORMATION
a. Overview
The referees, assistant referees, and fourth officials (when applicable) will be assigned for each
Match by Operator utilizing a designated Referee Management Group.
b. Fees
Referee fees will be paid as set forth in Section IX(l).
c. Match Report
After each Match, the referee shall complete the official Match Report form. The referee shall
enter the game data using the online reporting system designated by Operator within 24 hours of
the Match. The Clubs will be responsible for reviewing the online Match Report and submitting
corrections to the appropriate Member in a timely matter. The Match Report will be used to
generate standings, track suspensions, and record statistics.
d. Match Information
On the Match Report, the referee shall note all occurrences of significance, such as injury (serious,
any suspected head injury, or any injury that requires emergency medical attention), misconduct
of Players leading to caution or expulsion, unsporting behavior by supporters and/or by Club
Representatives, or any other person acting on behalf of a Club at the Match and any other incident
happening before, during, and after the Match in as much detail as possible.
XVII. MISCELLANEOUS
a. Special Circumstances
Operator, in its sole and absolute discretion, will issue any instructions necessitated by special
circumstances that may arise. These instructions shall form an integral part of these Rules and
Regulations and may, in exceptional circumstances, override a specific regulation.
b. Matters Not Provided For
Matters not provided for in these Rules and Regulations, and cases of force majeure shall be
decided by Operator and/or PDev. All decisions will be final.
c. Audit of Club
Operator reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to audit a Club to ensure compliance with any
and all Rules and Policies.
27

of the information contained in these MLS NEXT Academy Division Rules and Regulations. I
certify that if I have not understood any information contained in these Rules, I have sought and
received an explanation of the information prior to signing this statement. I further certify that I
will ensure that all relevant Club Representatives will be required to read these Rules.
